Center: US National Nuclear Data Center, Brookhaven, USA
ref: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.110, p.025806 (2024)
title: Energy, strength, and alpha width measurements of Ec.m. = 1323 and 1487 keV resonances in 15N(alpha,gamma)19F

x4freetext: Energy, strength, and alpha width measurements of
Ec.m. = 1323 and 1487 keV resonances in
15N(alpha,gamma)19F

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.110, p.025806 (2024)

x4codes.hlp: University of Notre Dame, Notre Dame, IN, United States of America
University of Notre Dame, Notre Dame, IN, United States of America

x4freetext: 5 MV Stable ion Accelerator for
Nuclear Astrophysics (Sta. ANA).
Gamma rays were detected using a high purity
germanium detector with a relative efficiency of 104%.
The Ti 15N target was fabricated
at the Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe through reactive
sputtering of Ti in a 99.5% enriched 15N environment.
The energy loss of the beam in the target at Ec.m. =
1323 keV was deduced from the excitation function to
be 11.7+-0.7 keV in the center of mass frame using
the full width at half maximum (FWHM) from the thick
target yield. The stability of the target was
verified by checking the yield of the Ec.m. = 1323
keV resonance at various times throughout the
experiment. No target material loss was observed
during the close distance measurements. However, a
23% reduction in Ti 15N content was observed after
depositing 0.7 C of beam from the longer duration,
far distance, measurements at Ec.m. = 1323 keV.
Approved by R.Fang on March 6, 2025.
BP
Yield per incident alpha particle.
The excitation functions over the Ec.m. =
1323 keV resonance in the 15N(alpha,gamma) 19F
reaction was measured. The energy spectrum measured
by the HPGe detector when populating the Ex = 5337
keV state. To mitigate angular distribution effects,
the detector was positioned at an angle of 55 degree
relative to the beam axis, as this angle corresponds
to the minimum of the second-order Legendre polynomial.
The statistical uncertainty of the yield.
There is an unknown uncertainty in the center of mass
energy.

x4freetext: Yield per incident alpha particle.
The excitation functions over the Ec.m. =
1487 keV resonance in the 15N(alpha,gamma) 19F
reaction was measured. The energy spectrum measured
by the HPGe detector when populating the Ex = 5501
keV state. To mitigate angular distribution effects,
the detector was positioned at an angle of 55 degree
relative to the beam axis, as this angle corresponds
to the minimum of the second-order Legendre polynomial.
The statistical uncertainty of the yield.
There is an unknown uncertainty in the center of mass
energy.

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.595, p.1 (1995)

x4freetext: Resonance strength
accepted value.
The energies of the resonances are
calculated using gamma energies and applying weighted
averaging, not the yield curve. The excitation
functions for both resonances were fitted using the
least square fit (LSF) method with the free
parameters: resonance strength, width, and Thetatarget.
The uncertainties of resonance strengths were
estimated using a Monte Carlo (MC) procedure. The
excitation functions were fitted numerous times with
the value of both the target thickness and the
stopping power of the beam being randomly generated
on a Gaussian distribution with their systematic
uncertainty as the standard deviation. The standard
deviation of the distributions of the fitted
parameters resulting from 10 000 iterations was added
quadratically to the statistical uncertainty of the
original LSF resulting in the total fitting procedure
uncertainty on those parameters.
Resonance strength systematic uncertainties.
MC procedure.
Branching ratio.
Detector efficiency.
Summing effect.
Charge collection.
Doppler shift corrected photopeak energies.
